title: Chicken & Garlic Parmesan
description: A luxurious stovetop meal featuring seared chicken thighs and macaroni tossed in a rich, creamy garlic pan sauce finished with Parmigiano Reggiano.
steps: Season the chicken thighs generously with salt and pepper.
Heat 15 ml of olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the chicken and cook for 5–6 minutes per side until golden brown and cooked through.
Remove the chicken to a plate to rest. Do not wipe the pan; leave the brown bits (fond) and rendered fat in the skillet.
Meanwhile, boil 225 g of macaroni in salted water, draining it 1 minute early so it remains al dente. Crucially, scoop out and reserve roughly 60–100 ml of the starchy pasta water before draining.
Reduce the skillet heat to medium. Add 15 g of butter to the chicken fat, then add the minced shallot and sauté for 1 minute until soft. Add the garlic and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Pour in 120 ml of white wine to deglaze the pan, scraping up all the brown bits from the bottom. Let the liquid reduce by half (about 2 minutes).
Whisk in 120 ml of chicken stock and 5 ml of Dijon mustard. Simmer for 2 minutes. Do not add extra salt at this stage.
Pour in 120 ml of heavy cream and bring to a gentle simmer. Add the drained macaroni and roughly 60 ml of the reserved pasta water directly into the sauce.
Toss everything constantly for 1 minute until the sauce thickens and coats the pasta.
Turn the heat completely OFF and move the pan off the hot burner. Sprinkle in the 70 g of grated Parmigiano Reggiano in two batches, stirring vigorously after each addition until melted and glossy.
Slice the rested chicken thighs and return them to the pan. Top with fresh parsley and plenty of black pepper before serving.
